title: PUBLIC SAFETY POWER SHUTOFFS - 211info
text: EUGENE WATER AND ELECTRIC BOARD PUBLIC SAFETY POWER SHUTOFFS Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF A Public Safety Power Shutoff (PSPS) is an operational practice an electric utility may use to shut off power in high-risk areas to help prevent wildfires during extreme and dangerous weather conditions. PSPS means EWEB will shut off power, and it is considered an action of last resort to help protect public safety. 4200 Roosevelt Boulevard Eugene, OR 97402 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: 24 hours per day / 7 days per week Email (541) 685-7000 Get Directions Visit Website Phone/FAX Numbers (541) 685-7000 Main phone Main phone (541) 685-7000 Toll-free (800) 841-5871 Email Address: [email protected] INTAKE PROCEDURE: Visit website for more information FEES: None DOCUMENTS: None AREA SERVED: Cities of Eugene, Leaburg, Springfield, Vida, and Walterville Services: Electric Service Connection/Repair * Disaster/Emergency Issues Other Services Offered At This Location: HOUSING/SHELTER - INCOME-BASED EFFICIENCY REBATES AND LOANS UTILITIES - EUGENE WATER AND ELECTRIC BOARD If you represent this agency, update your listing here . * The community resource directory information is up to date to the best of our knowledge. However, you should always call the provider to confirm this information and make an appointment. Be sure to confirm payment information with the provider, if payment is required. 211Info does not rate, recommend or endorse any agency. We simply provide information as a public service. EUGENE WATER AND ELECTRIC BOARD
